meaning: n.(浠庡叾浠栦汉鍙ｅ垎鍑烘垨鏋勬垚鎬讳汉鍙ｇ殑)鍒嗙粍浜哄彛
examples: Researchers have found a large subpopulation of the insect that prefers to live outside.
This means that a new growth niche is made available to a subpopulation of the parasites.
Experimental study demonstrated that this recipe can regulate T-lymphocyte subpopulation and induce the production of IF.
The greatest damage from Vioxx may have been to the subpopulation of arthritis sufferers already predisposed to having a heart attack.
You would try to treat as many people as possible and hope that you could identify a subpopulation of people that would respond to your drug.
